title: sida subcordata
intro: Sida subcordata is a type of plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the cottonwood or cotton rose. It is native to tropical regions and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Sida subcordata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
